label: RECIPE_TITLE
text: Pork Crackling with Bramley Apple Sauce

label: INGREDIENT_LINE
text: 1 kg pork rind, with a 5mm layer of fat left on

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Preheat the oven to 200°c. Cut the pork rind into rough strips, about 12cm long and 3cm wide. Place in a pan of cold water, bring to a boil and simmer for about 15 minutes. Drain well, place on a baking tray and scatter with 1 tbsp sea salt.

label: OTHER
text: Roast for about 1 hour until the rind is crisp, turning and draining the fat from the tray every so often (save it to roast potatoes)

label: OTHER
text: Meanwhile, make the apple sauce. Peel, quarter and core the apple, then cut into chunks and place in a heavy-based pan over a medium heat. Add the sugar and cook for about 10 minutes, stirring every so often, until the apple has broken up. Remove from the heat you can give the sauce a bit of a whisk to make it smoother 1f you wish, but i like it quite chunky. Transfer to individual serving bowls

label: OTHER
text: Drain the pork in a colander and scatter over some more salt while hot. Serve the crackling on a platter, with the apple sauce as a dip on the side.

label: OTHER
text: 6-8 garlic cloves, peeled and sliced 200g butter

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Clean the ceps with a cloth and make about 5 incisions in each one with the point of a knife. Push a slice of garlic into each incisions

label: OTHER
text: Melt half of the butter in a roasting tray add the ceps, turn to coat them in the butter and season generously with salt and pepper roast in the oven for 20-30 minutes until the ceps are iust tender, turning them every so often to ensure they cook and colour evenly

label: OTHER
text: Add the rest of the butter and the chopped parsley, stir well and return to the oven for 2 3 minutes

label: INGREDIENT_LINE
text: 2-3 good-quality pickled walruts chopped plus 2 tbsp of their juice

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Whisk all of the ingredients together in a bowl or shake in a screw-topped jar or bottle, seasoning with salt and pepper to taste

label: INGREDIENT_LINE
text: 4 tbsp cold pressed rape seed oil

label: OTHER
text: Crush blackberries, then whisk in vin/oil

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Whisk the cider vinegar, mustard and rapeseed oil together in a bowl or shake in a screw-topped jar

label: OTHER
text: Or bottle. Season the dressing with salt and pepper to taste

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Whisk the cider vinegar, honey, mustard and oils together in a bowl or shake in a screw-topped jar or bottle. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Whisk the cider vinegar, lemon juice, garlic, tarragon and oils together or shake in a screw-topped jar or

label: OTHER
text: Bottle. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Leave to infuse overnight and strain before using.

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Blend the cider vinegar, mustard, blue cheese and oils together in a blender and season with salt and pepper to taste. If the dressing is too thick, thin it with a little water

label: OTHER
text: 2 young fennel bulbs, trimmed and green tops reserved

label: OTHER
text: Sea salt and freshly ground white pepper

label: OTHER
text: Halve the fennel bulbs and slice them as thinly as possible, using a mandolin or very sharp knife. Tip into a bowl, add the cider vinegar and season

label: OTHER
text: To taste. Toss to mix and leave for about an hour, stirring every so often

label: OTHER
text: Chop the green fennel tops and add to the fennel with the rapeseed oil. Toss to combine, then taste and adjust the seasoning if necessary. Serve within a couple of hours.

label: OTHER
text: Broad bean, pea and girolle salad

label: OTHER
text: A few sprigs of tarragon, leaves removed and stalks reserved

label: OTHER
text: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: A handful of pea shoots (if available)

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Put the garlic, rapeseed oil and tarragon stalks into a wide saucepan with about 3 tbsp water. Season well and bring to the boil. Add the girolles, cover with a lid and cook over a low heat for 3-4 minutes, turning them with a spoon every so often. Take off the heat, remove the lid and leave to cool a little. Cook the peas and broad beans separately in boiling salted water for a few minutes until just tender, adding a little caster sugar to the cooking water for the peas. Drain and remove the tough skins from any larger broad beans; leave small ones as they are. Remove the girolles from their liquid with a slotted

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Spoon and set aside on a plate. To make the dressing, carefully pour off the oily part of the girolle cooking liquid into a bowl, leaving the watery liquid behind in the pan. Whisk the cider vinegar into the oil. Chop the tarragon leaves and stir these in, too. Check the seasoning. Toss the peas and broad beans in the dressing, then spoon onto serving plates. Scatter the girolles on top, along with a few pea shoots, if using. Spoon a little more dressing over the salad and serve.

label: RECIPE_TITLE
text: Potato Salad with Bacon and Spring Onions

label: OTHER
text: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: 1 medium onion, peeled and finely chopped

label: OTHER
text: 3 rashers of rind less streaky bacon finely chopped

label: OTHER
text: 4 spring onions, trimmed and finely chopped

label: OTHER
text: Cook the potatoes in a pan of boiling salted water for 12-15 minutes until just tender. Drain and leave until cool enough to handle.

label: OTHER
text: Meanwhile, put the onion, bacon, cumin seeds, cider vinegar and chicken stock into a saucepan and simmer gently until the liquid has reduced by about

label: OTHER
text: Two-thirds, then take off the heat.

label: OTHER
text: Peel the potatoes and cut into 3mm thick slices. Add to the onion and bacon mixture, along with the rapeseed oil. Toss gently to mix and season with salt

label: OTHER
text: Cover and leave to stand for up to an hour until ready to serve, giving an occasional stir to encourage the potatoes to absorb all the flavours. Just before

label: OTHER
text: Serving, add the spring onions and toss gently

label: INGREDIENT_LINE
text: 1 small leek, trimmed, cut into lcm dice and washed

label: INGREDIENT_LINE
text: 1 celery stick, peeled if necessary and cut into 5mm dice

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: 60g green or runner beans, cut into 5mm lengths

label: OTHER
text: Pour the vegetable stock into a large saucepan, add the leek and celery and bring to a simmer. Season lightly with salt and pepper and let simmer for

label: OTHER
text: Meanwhile, blanch the broad beans in boiling salted water for 3-4 minutes, then drain and refresh under the cold tap. Remove the tough skins from any larger

label: OTHER
text: Broad beans; leave small ones as they are.

label: OTHER
text: Add the peas and green beans to the simmering stock and cook for a further 10 minutes. Add the broad beans, chopped parsley and chives, and simmer for another 5 minutes. Re-season if necessary before serving.

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: 1 small onion, peeled and rough ly chopped

label: OTHER
text: Cream , creme fraiche or soft goat's cheese

label: OTHER
text: Cook the beetroot in a pan of salted water for about 1 hour until they feel tender when pierced with a knife. Drain and leave until cool enough to handle,

label: OTHER
text: Then peel and roughly chop the beetroot.

label: OTHER
text: Heat the oil in a large saucepan, add the onion, cover and cook gently for 3- 4 minutes to soften. Add the vegetable stock, bring to the boil, season with

label: OTHER
text: A little salt and pepper and simmer for 30 minutes.

label: OTHER
text: Add the beetroot, then remove from the heat. Puree in a blender until smooth, then pass the soup through a fine-meshed sieve into a large bowl. Re-season if necessary. Cool the soup down, then refrigerate for a few hours until well chilled or put it into the freezer if you're in a rush.

label: OTHER
text: Serve in soup plates topped with a dollop of cream, creme fraiche or soft goat's cheese and a sprinkling of grated horseradish.

label: OTHER
text: A good handful of parsley, washed

label: OTHER
text: A good handful of chervil , washed

label: OTHER
text: A good handful of basil, washed

label: OTHER
text: 1 medium onion, peeled and roughly chopped

label: OTHER
text: 1 leek, t rimmed, roughly chopped and washed

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Goat's curd, soft goat's cheese or creme fralche

label: OTHER
text: Finely chopped herbs, such as chervil, chives and

label: OTHER
text: Break off the thicker stalks from the parsley, chervil and basil and set aside; keep the leaves separate.

label: OTHER
text: Heat the rapeseed oil 1n a large saucepan and add the fennel , onion and leek. Cover and cook gently for 4-5 minutes until soft, stirring every so often.

label: OTHER
text: Add the flour and stir well, then gradually stir in the vegetable stock. Season to taste.

label: OTHER
text: Bring to the boil, add the herb stalks and simmer gently for 20 minutes. Add the rest of the herbs, including the chives and mint leaves. Simmer for

label: OTHER
text: Puree the soup in a blender until smooth, then pass through a medium sieve (rather than a fine one, to let the herb bits th rough) into a bowl.

label: OTHER
text: Set the bowl over another la rger bowl of iced water to cool the soup down as quickly as possible (this helps to preserve its green colour). Re-season if necessary.

label: OTHER
text: Serve the soup straight from the fridge. If you like, top with a dollop of goat's curd, goat's cheese or creme fraiche and a sprinkling of chopped herbs

label: OTHER
text: 1 large leek, t rimmed, roughly chopped and washed

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Loog freshly grated horseradish, or more to taste

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Melt the butter in a large saucepan. Add the onion and leek, cover and cook gently without colouring for 3-4 minutes, stirring every so often, to soften stir in the flour and cook over a low heat for 30 seconds, then gradually stir in the vegetable stock. Bring to the boil, season and simmer for 30 minutes.

label: OTHER
text: Add about two-thirds of the horseradish to the soup and simmer for 5 minutes, then take off the heat. Puree in a blender until smooth, then pass through

label: OTHER
text: A fine-meshed sieve into a clean saucepan.

label: OTHER
text: Taste for seasoning and the horseradish, adding more to taste. You don't need to blend the soup again the extra horseradish gives a nice te lure. Add the

label: OTHER
text: Cream, bring back to a simmer and serve.

label: RECIPE_TITLE
text: Ling with Creamed Peas, Leeks and Bacon

label: INGREDIENT_LINE
text: 4 thick f illets of ling, about 200g each, with skin salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: L50g freshly podded peas (about 300g before podding). Or frozen peas

label: OTHER
text: 6 rashers of streaky bacon, derinded and finely chopped

label: OTHER
text: 1 medium or 2 small leeks, trimmed, cut into rough 1 cm squares and washed

label: OTHER
text: Season the ling fillets with salt and pepper. Add the peas to a pan of salted water and simmer for 4-5 minutes or until tender, then drain.

label: OTHER
text: In the meantime, melt half of the butter in a heavybased saucepan and add the bacon and leek. Cover and cook gently over a medium-low heat for 3-4

label: OTHER
text: Minutes, stirring every so often, until the leek is soft.

label: OTHER
text: Roughly chop the peas and add them to the leek and bacon with the cream. Season with salt and pepper and simmer for a few minutes until the cream has

label: OTHER
text: Reduced and is just coating the peas.

label: OTHER
text: Meanwhile, heat the oil in a non-stick frying pan over a medium-high heat and add the ling fillets, skin side down. Cook for 3-4 minutes until nicely

label: OTHER
text: Coloured, then turn the fillets and add the rest of the butter to the pan. Fry for a couple of minutes until the fish is just cooked through.

label: OTHER
text: To serve, spoon the creamed peas, leeks and bacon onto warmed plates and place the fish on top.

label: OTHER
text: Thorn back ray with fried egg, capers and anchovies

label: OTHER
text: 4 fillets from a medium ray wing, about 170g each salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Warm the oven to its lowest setting. Season the ray fillets with salt and pepper. Have 3 dishes ready, one with the flour, one with the beaten egg and the third with the breadcrumbs. One at a time, coat the fish fillets in the flour, shaking off any excess, then pass through the egg and finally coat in the breadcrumbs.

label: OTHER
text: You will probably need to cook the fish in a couple of batches. Heat the oil in a large frying pan and fry the ray fillets for 2-3 minutes on each side, adding a knob of butter once you've turned them over. Keep warm in a low oven.

label: OTHER
text: Melt a little more butter in a non-stick frying pan and gently fry the eggs for a couple of minutes until the white has just set.

label: OTHER
text: Heat the remaining butter in a pan until foaming, add the capers and chopped parsley and take off the heat. Lay a ray fillet on each warmed plate, place

label: OTHER
text: A fried egg on top and arrange the anchovy strips around the egg white. Spoon the butter and capers over the egg and serve.

label: OTHER
text: 1 garlic clove, peeled and crushed, or a few wild garlic leaves

label: OTHER
text: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Put the herb leaves, capers, garlic and mustard into a blender with most of the rapeseed oil and process to a fairly course puree season with salt and pepper to taste and add a little more oil if the sauce seems a little dry.

label: OTHER
text: 1 red onion, peeled and finely chopped

label: OTHER
text: 1 tbsp henderson's relish or worcestershire sauce

label: OTHER
text: Heat the rapeseed oil in a pan and add the onion, garlic and pepper cook gently for 2-3 minutes to soften, then tip into a bowl. Add all of the other

label: OTHER
text: Spoon the flavoured butter onto a sheet of cling film or greaseproof paper and shape into a cylinder, about 3cm in diameter, sealing it in the film or paper

label: OTHER
text: Just slice the chilled butter as you need it and serve directly on steaks and chops; or cut off as much frozen butter as you need and slice it before serving

label: INGREDIENT_LINE
text: 1 tbsp white wine or cider vinegar

label: INGREDIENT_LINE
text: 1 small shallot, peeled and roughly chopped

label: OTHER
text: A few sprigs of tarragon, leaves picked, stalks reserved

label: OTHER
text: A few sprigs of chervil, leaves picked, stalks reserved

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground white pepper

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Put the vinegar, 2 tbsp water, the shallot, herb stalks and peppercorns in a saucepan and simmer for a minute or until the liquid has reduced to 2 tsp. Strain through a sieve and leave to cool.

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Melt the butter in a saucepan over a low heat and let bubble very gently for 3-4 minutes. Remove from the heat, leave to cool a little, then pour off the pure liquid butter into a bowl, leaving the milky whey behind. Discard the whey. (clarifying the butter in this way helps to keep the sauce thick.)

label: OTHER
text: Put the egg yolks into a small heatproof bowl (or a double boiler if you have one) with half of the vinegar reduction and set over a pan of gently simmering water. Whisk the mixture until it begins to thicken and become frothy. Slowly trickle in two-thirds of the clarified butter, whisking continuously - preferably using an electric hand whisk. If the butter is added too quickly, the sauce is liable to separate.

label: OTHER
text: Now taste the sauce and add a little more, or all, of the remaining vinegar reduction. Then whisk in the rest of the butter. The vinegar should just cut the oiliness of the butter sauce; don't add too much. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

label: OTHER
text: Cover the bowl and leave in a warm, not hot, place until needed, but no longer than 20 minutes.

label: OTHER
text: To serve, chop the tarragon and chervil leaves and stir into the sauce check the seasoning and add a squeeze of lemon juice, to taste. Whisk lightly

label: OTHER
text: 2-3 handfuls of wild garlic leaves. Washed and dried

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Put the wild garlic leaves. Mustard and looml rapeseed oil into a blender and process as smoothly or as coarsely as you wish. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Add as much of the extra oil as you need to get the desired consistency.

label: OTHER
text: Roast Chicken with New Season's Garlic Sauce

label: OTHER
text: 1 free-range chicken, about l.5kg, with livers sea salt and freshly ground black pepper a few sprigs each of thyme and rosemary, a few generous knobs of butter

label: OTHER
text: 1 medium onion , peeled and finely chopped

label: OTHER
text: Preheat the oven to 200°c. Season the chicken inside and out. Put the herbs into the cavity. Rub butter all over the breast and legs.

label: OTHER
text: For the stuffing, melt the butter in a pan. Add the onion, livers and thyme, season and cook over a medium heat for 2-3 minutes. Off the heat, mix in the breadcrumbs, parsley and seasoning. Either use to stuff your bird or cook separately in an oven proof dish or wrapped in foil for the last 30-40 minutes.

label: OTHER
text: Put the chicken into a large roasting tin and roast in the oven, basting regularly and adding the livers to the roasting tin for the last 6 minutes or so. Test the chicken after 1 1/4 hours by inserting a skewer into the thickest part of the thigh. The juices should run clear; if not roast for a little longer

label: OTHER
text: Lift the chicken onto a warmed platter and rest in a warm place for 15 minutes spnnkle with chopped parsley and serve with the roasted livers, stuffing, garlic sauce and straw potatoes.

label: INGREDIENT_LINE
text: 4 heads of new season's garlic a few sprigs of curly parsley

label: INGREDIENT_LINE
text: 1/2 tbsp dijon mustard, or more to taste

label: INGREDIENT_LINE
text: 2-3 tbsp duck fat, warmed (or pan juices from a roasted chicken)

label: OTHER
text: 70g fresh white breadcrumbs a ltttle milk to mix

label: OTHER
text: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Preheat the oven to 200 c. Wrap the garlic bulbs in foil and bake them in the oven for 1hour. Unwrap and leave until cool enough to handle, then peel away any tough outer skin.

label: OTHER
text: Put the garlic into a blender with the parsley, mustard, warm duck fat (or chicken pan juices) and breadcrumbs and blend until smooth add enough milk to give the sauce a thick pouring consistency and season with salt/pepper

label: OTHER
text: A couple of knobs of butter

label: OTHER
text: L small onion, peeled and finely chopped

label: OTHER
text: L garlic clove, peeled and crushed a good pinch of cayenne pepper

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: 1 large dill pickle or a few gherkins, finely diced

label: OTHER
text: Melt the butter in a heavy-based pan and add the onion, garlic and cayenne pepper cook gently for 2-3 minutes to soften, then stir in the flour, followed

label: OTHER
text: By the tomato puree, cider vinegar, redcurrant jelly and mustard.

label: OTHER
text: 500g boned and skinned free-range chicken thighs

label: OTHER
text: A good pinch of saffron strands

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Remove the meat from the lobster tails and claws and cut roughly into lcm pieces. Reserve one lobster head (if making a large pie). Break the rest of the shells up a bit, using a heavy knife. Cut the chicken thighs in half, or into thirds if large. Cover and refrigerate the lobster and chicken meat.

label: OTHER
text: To make the sauce, heat the oil in a heavy based saucepan and fry the lobster shells, shallots and garlic over a medium heat for about 5 minutes until they begin to colour lightly add the butter and, once melted, stir in the flour. Add the saffron, tarragon and tomato puree, then gradually stir in the white wine and the hot fish and chicken stocks.

label: OTHER
text: Bring to the boil, lower the heat and simmer for about 30 minutes until the sauce has reduced by about half, then add the cream. Season lightly with salt and pepper, bring back to the boil and simmer very gently for about 20 minutes until the sauce has reduced by half again. (a simmer plate or heat diffuser mat is useful here.)

label: OTHER
text: Strain the sauce through a colander into a clean pan, movingthe shells with a spoon to ensure all the sauce goes through.

label: OTHER
text: Tip about one-tenth of the shells into a blender and add about a cupful of the strained sauce. Blend until smooth,then strain through a fine· meshed sieve rnto the sauce in the pan.

label: OTHER
text: Bring the sauce back to the boil, add the chicken and simmer for 5 minutes. The sauce should be a thick coating consistency by now; if not, simmer

label: OTHER
text: A little longer (or dilute a little cornflour in water and stir into the sauce) leave to cool.

label: OTHER
text: Stir the lobster and chopped parsley and tarragon into the cooled sauce adjust the seasoning if necessary.fill a large pre dishor 4 1ndiv1dual ones with the mixture.

label: OTHER
text: Roll out the pastry on a lightly floured surface to a 5mm thickness.trim to about 2cm larger all round than the pie dish (or cut discs large enough to cover individual dishes). Brush the edges of the pastry with a little of the beaten egg.lay the pastry over the filling, pressing the egg-washed sides onto the rim of the dish(es).

label: OTHER
text: If making a large pie, cut a cross in the centre and insert the reserved lobster head, so 1t sits proud. Cut a small slit in the top of 1nd1v1dual pies to allow steam to escape.leave to rest in a cool place for 30 minutes.

label: OTHER
text: Preheat the oven to 200°c. Brush the pastry lid with beaten egg and bake the pie for 40-50 minutes or until golden brown (allow 1o-15 minutes less for individual pies). Let the pie stand for a few minutes before serving

label: RECIPE_TITLE
text: Mixed Grill of Pork with Crab Apple Sauce

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: 200-250g shoulder or neck fillet of pork

label: OTHER
text: 1 tbsp caster sugar, or more to taste

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: First make the crab apple sauce. Remove the stalks from the apples and wash them well put them into a heavy- based saucepan with ltbsp sugar. Cover and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring every so often until the apples are soft. If there's a lot of liquid, remove the lid and boil rapidly until it dries out enough, takingcare that it doesn't splatter. Transfer to a bowl and taste for sweetness, adding a little more sugar if needed.spoon into individual serving bowls and let cool to room temperature

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: In a bowl, mix the minced pork with the black pudding and season well with salt and pepper divide into 4 portions, flatten and shape into patties cover and place in the fridge. Cut the pork loin into 4 similar-sized chunks; do the same with the pork shoulder, kidneys and liver.season all of the meat.

label: OTHER
text: Heat a ridged griddle pan or heavy-based frying pan and lightly oil it. Cook the pork patties and pieces of meat and offal 1n batches as necessary, for about 3-4 minutes on each side, keeping them slightly pink.the liver and kidney will need less time, just 1-2 minutes.

label: OTHER
text: Heat the butter and rosemary in a separate pan until foaming and remove from the heat. Thread the meat onto skewers or simply arrange on warmed plates. Trickle the butter over the meat and serve. With the crab apple sauce.

label: HOWTO_SECTION
text: For the roasted curry spice mix

label: INGREDIENT_LINE
text: 1/2 tbsp cardamom seeds (from green cardamom pods)

label: OTHER
text: To prepare the spice mix, grind all of the spices, except the ground cumin, cinnamon and coriander, in a spice grinder or with a mortar and pestle. Then mix them with the ready-ground spices and sprinkle into a heavy-based frying pan. Cook over a medium heat, stirring constantly, until they turn dark brown; don't let them burn. Tip onto a plate and leave to cool, then store in a sealed jar until needed

label: OTHER
text: 75g ghee (or half oil, half butter mix)

label: OTHER
text: 2 medium red onions, peeled and finely chopped

label: OTHER
text: A small piece of fresh root ginger, peeled and finely grated

label: OTHER
text: A good pinch of saffron strands

label: OTHER
text: A good handful of curry leaves

label: OTHER
text: 2 tbsp roasted curry spice mix (see left)

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: A few sprigs of coriander, roughly chopped

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Coat the mutton chops with the yoghurt and leave to marinate for a couple of hours.

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Meanwhile, heat two thirds of the ghee in a pan and gently cook the onions with the garlic, ginger, saffron and a few of the curry leaves for 3-4 minutes until softened. Add the roasted curry spice mix and tomato puree and stir well. Pour in the stock and bring to the boil, then lower the heat and let the sauce simmer for 20 minutes.

label: OTHER
text: Transfer the sauce to a blender and process until smooth,then strain through a fine-meshed sieve into a clean pan, pushing as much through as possible. Return to a low heat and simmer until the sauce has reduced and thickened .

label: OTHER
text: Heat the remaining ghee in a frying pan.remove excess yoghurt from the chops and season them, then fry until lightly coloured on both sides. Pour the sauce over the chops and simmer gently for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add the coriander and simmer for another couple of minutes.

label: OTHER
text: Meanwhile, heat the oil in a frying pan and briefly fry the rest of the curry leaves.scatter over the curry and serve with basmati rice.

label: RECIPE_TITLE
text: Lamb Chops with Cucumber and Mint

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Vegetable or corn oil for brushing

label: OTHER
text: 2 large shallots, peeled, halved and thinly sliced

label: OTHER
text: A small handful of mint leaves, shredded

label: OTHER
text: Cut the cucumbers in half lengthways and scoop out the seeds with a teaspoon. Cut each half on an angle into 1-2cm thick slices.

label: OTHER
text: Heat a ridged griddle pan or heavy-based fryingpan over a medium-high heat. Season the lamb chops with salt and pepper, brush with oil and cook for about 5 minutes on each side, keeping them pink.

label: OTHER
text: Meanwhile, heat the rapeseed oil in another frying pan and add the shallots.fry,stirring, for a minute, then add the cucumber slices. Season with salt

label: OTHER
text: And pepper and saute over a fairly high heat for 2-3 minutes until tender. Take off the heat and stir in the shredded mint.

label: OTHER
text: Divide the cucumber between warmed plates and place the lamb chops on top to serve

label: OTHER
text: Lamb's sweetbreads with runner beans. Bacon and mushrooms

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: 120g piece of unsmoked streaky bacon, cut into 5mm dice

label: OTHER
text: L50g g1rolles or other seasonal wild mushrooms, cleaned

label: OTHER
text: 1 small red onion, peeled and finely chopped

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Pul the sweetbreads into a pan of cold salted water. Bring to the boil and lower the heat. Simmer for 2-3 minutes, then drain and leave to cool.

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Meanwhile, for the dressing, put the red onion, cider vinegar and 2 tbsp water into a small pan and simmer for 2-3 minutes or untilthe liquid has reduced by half remove from the heat and whisk in the mustard and rapeseed oil. Season with salt and pepper and set aside.

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Shred the runner beans lengthways on a slight angle very finely. Add to a saucepan of boilingsalted water and cook for 30 seconds, then drain in a co1ander. Refresh briefly under the cold tap, drain and pat dry with kitchen paper.

label: OTHER
text: Trim the blanched sweetbreads of any fat and membrane, then season them. Melt half the butter in a frying pan and cook the sweetbreads .stirring frequently, over a fairly high heat for 4-5 minutes until golden. Drain on kitchen paper and keep warm

label: OTHER
text: Melt the rest of the butter in the frying pan add the bacon pieces and fry over a medium heat for 2-3 minutes,then add the mushrooms cook for 4-5 minutes until tender, turning them every so often and lightly seasoning them

label: OTHER
text: Meanwhile, warm the runner beans in 1tbsp rapeseed oil, then toss in the dressing and season

label: OTHER
text: To serve, divide the runner beans between warmed serving plates and scatter the sweetbreads and mushrooms over the top. Serve at once

label: RECIPE_TITLE
text: Crosscut Ribs Baked in a Barbecue Sauce

label: OTHER
text: A couple of generous knobs of butter

label: OTHER
text: 4 large shallots, peeled and finely chopped

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Preheat the oven to l50°c. For the barbecue sauce, simply mix all the ingredients together in a bowl, seasoning with salt and pepper to taste.

label: OTHER
text: Heat 1tbsp oil in a large, heavy-based frying pan over a medium-high heat. When hot, fry the ribs in batches for 2-3 minutes on each side until they are nicely coloured, adding more oil to the pan as necessary. Once browned, transfer the ribs to a large ovenproof dish.

label: OTHER
text: Pour the barbecue sauce over the ribs and toss to coat all over. Cook in the oven for about an hour or until the ribs are tender and glazed, and the meat is 1ust falling away from the bone.

label: OTHER
text: 8 large courgette, squash or pumpkin flowers, with their stalks

label: OTHER
text: 120g self-raising flour, plus extra for dusting

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Vegetable or corn oil for deep-frying

label: OTHER
text: Check that the flowers are clean and free from insects. To make the batter, place the flour in a bowl and slowly whisk in 200ml ice-cold water to form a thickish batter. Add the grated cheese, season with salt and pepper and give it a final whisk.

label: OTHER
text: Heat an 8cm depth of oil in a deep-fat fryer or other suitable deep, heavy pan to 160-180°c, or until a little batter dropped into the oil turns golden brown after a minute or so.

label: OTHER
text: Have a shallow bowl of flour ready. One at a time, dip the flowers into the flour, shaking off any excess, then dip into the batter to coat and drop into the

label: OTHER
text: Hot oil. Deep-fry 3 or 4 flowers at a time, moving them around in the oil with a spoon, for a couple of minutes until they are crisp and light golden.

label: OTHER
text: Remove the courgette flowers with a slotted spoon and put on a plate lined with kitchen paper to drain. Season lightly with salt and serve.

label: OTHER
text: 1 head of green cabbage, or a mixture of greens

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: 1 small onion, peeled and finely chopped

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Cut the cabbage or greens roughly into 2-3cm chunks and wash well. Add to a pan of boiling salted water, bring back to the boil and cook for 3-5 minutes until just tender. Drain thoroughly in a colander.

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Meanwhile, melt half of the butter in a frying pan and gently cook the onion and garlic for 3-4 minutes to soften.

label: OTHER
text: Add the rest of the butter to the pan. Once melted, add the mushrooms and cook over a medium heat for 4-5 minutes until tender, turning frequently and seasoning after a couple of minutes.

label: OTHER
text: Add the cabbage to the mushrooms and toss them together over the heat until hot. Check the seasoning and serve at once.

label: OTHER
text: About 100-120ml scrumpy or cider to mix

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Vegetable or corn oil for deep-frying

label: OTHER
text: Cut the yellow onion into 5mm thick rings;cut each spring onion into 4 lengths; halve the red onion and cut into 5mm thick half-moon slices. Separate the slices and rings.

label: OTHER
text: To make the batter, put the flour into a bowl and whisk in enough of the cider to make a smooth, thick batter. Season with salt and pepper. Leavelo rest for about 30 minutes.

label: OTHER
text: Heat an 8cm depth of oil in a deep-fat fryer or other suitable deep,heavy pan to 160-180°c. Season the flour for dusting well.

label: OTHER
text: To test that the batter is the correct thickness,dust a piece of onion in flour, then coat in batter and

label: OTHER
text: Immerse in the hot 011. The batter should adhere and the onionshould cook up crisp; if not, adjust the batter with more cider or flour.

label: OTHER
text: You'll need to cook the onions in 2 or 3 batches. Toss the first batch in the flour, shaking off excess, then dip into the batter to coat and drop into the hot oil. Deep fry for about 3-4 minutes until nicely coloured, turning them occasionally with a slotted spoon. Drain on kitchen paper, then scatter with onion salt. Eat straight away or keep hot while you cook the rest, then serve at once.

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Preheat the oven to 200 c. Cut the cauliflower into large florets. Pour the milk into a saucepan, season and bring to the boil add the cauliflower florets and cook for 7-8 minutes or until just tender. Drain in a colander over a bowl, reserving the milk.

label: OTHER
text: Melt the butter in a heavy-based saucepan, then stir in the flour and cook, stirring,over a low heat for 30 seconds. Gradually whisk in the milk, keeping the mixture smooth. Add the bay leaf, re-season and simmer very gently for about 15 minutes, stirring from time to time.

label: OTHER
text: Add the cream and simmer for a further 5 minutes the sauce should be quite thick by now: if not simmer gently until it is a thick coating consistency. Remove the bay leaf, then stir in three quarters of the grated cheese and the egg yolk.

label: OTHER
text: Mix the cauliflower with the sauce and transfer to an ovenproof serving dish. Scatter the rest of the cheese over the surface and bake in the oven for about 15 minutes or until nicely browned

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Roughly chop the runner beans. Bring a pan of salted water to the boil. Drop in the beans and cook for 5-7 minutes until iust tender, then drain

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Tip the beans into a blender or food processor and blend to a puree as coarse or smooth as you like you may need to add a little water.

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Return the puree to the pan and place over a low heat. Add the butter, season with salt and pepper to taste, then serve.

label: OTHER
text: 300 400g freshly podded peas (about 1 kg before podding)

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: A handful of mint sprigs, leaves stripped, stalks reserved

label: OTHER
text: A couple of handfuls of pea shoots

label: OTHER
text: For the peas, bring a pan of salted water to the boil with the sugar and mint stalks added tip in the peas and simmer for 4-5 minutes or until tender. Then drain and discard the mint stalks.

label: KNOWLEDGE
text: Melt the butter in a wide pan add the pea shoots quickly season with salt and pepper and toss for a few seconds until they are just wilted.

label: OTHER
text: Tip in the cooked peas, toss to combine and re-season if necessary before serving

label: OTHER
text: 750-800g spinach, thick stalks removed and washed

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Bring a pan of salted water to the boil. Plunge in the spinach leaves and cook for 2-3 minutes until tender. Drain in a colander, refresh under the cold tap and squeeze out any excess water.

label: OTHER
text: Tip the spinach into a food processor and blend as smoothly or as coarsely as you wish. You can take it to this stage in advance if you like, then finish at the last minute.

label: OTHER
text: Simmer the cream in a saucepan until reduced by about half, then stir in the spinach and season with salt and pepper to taste. Cook over a low heat for a minute or so, stirring every so often, until the spinach is hot. Serve immediately

label: OTHER
text: Sprouting broccoli with her bs and hazelnuts

label: OTHER
text: A couple of generous knobs of butter

label: OTHER
text: 2-3 tbsp finely chopped mixed herbs, such

label: OTHER
text: As parsley, chervil, tarragon and chives

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: For the crust, preheat the grill to medium. In a bowl, toss the breadcrumbs and chopped hazelnuts together with the rapeseed oil and scatter on a baking tray. Toast under the grill for 3-5 minutes until golden,turningonce or twice to ensure the mixture colours evenly.

label: OTHER
text: Meanwhile, melt the butter in a pan and gently cook the shallots and garlic for a few minutes until softened. Stir in the breadcrumbs and herbs until well mixed and season with salt and pepper

label: OTHER
text: When ready to serve, cook the broccoli 1n boiling salted water for about 4-5 minutes, so that it is still just a little firm to the bite. Drain thoroughly and transfer to a warmed serving dish. Scatter the breadcrumb mixture over the top and serve.

label: OTHER
text: A good pinch of freshly grated nutmeg

label: OTHER
text: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

label: OTHER
text: Preheat the oven to 160 c. Cut the parsnips into rough 2-3cm chunks.

label: OTHER
text: Pour the cream and milk into a saucepan, add the nutmeg and garlic, and season generously with salt and pepper. Bring to the boil, then turn off the heat and leave to cool slightly.

label: OTHER
text: Put the parsnips into a shallow ovenproof (gratin type) dish and mix with all but 1tbsp of the cheese. Pour the cream mixture over the top.

label: OTHER
text: Stand the dish in a roasting tray and pour in enough boiling water to come halfway up the side of the dish (to create a bain-marie). Cook in the oven for about an hour until the parsnips are cooked through.

label: OTHER
text: Preheat the grill to medium. Mix the breadcrumbs with the chopped parsley and remaining 1tbsp grated cheese scatter over the parsnip bake and place under the grill for a few minutes until golden. Serve at once, or cover with foil and leave in a low oven until ready to serve.

label: OTHER
text: 4 large chipping potatoes, such as yukon gold, spunta or mans piper, peeled

label: OTHER
text: Vegetable or corn oil for deep-trying

label: OTHER
text: 10 garlic cloves, peeled and thinly sliced

label: OTHER
text: Using a mandolin with a shredding attachment or a sharp knife, cut the potatoes into long matchsticks, about 3mm thick. Wash them well in a couple of changes of cold water to remove excess starch, then drain and pat dry on a clean tea towel.

label: OTHER
text: Heat an 8cm depth of oil in a deep-fat fryer or other suitable deep, heavy pan to 120-140 c. Blanch the matchstick potatoes in manageable batches (a couple of handfuls at a time) for 2-3 minutes, then remove with a slotted spoon and drain in a colander

label: OTHER
text: Increase the temperatur e of the oil to 160-180-c and re-fry the potatoes with the garlic slices in batches, moving them around in the pan, until golden and crisp. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on kitchen paper. Sprinkle with sea salt and serve immediately.

label: OTHER
text: Alternatively, you can keep the straw potatoes warm, uncovered, on a baking tray in a low oven until ready to serve. They should stay crisp but if not you can briefly re-fry them in hot oil to crisp them up again.

label: OTHER
text: 250g good-quality dark chocolate, about 70% cocoa solids, broken into small pieces

label: OTHER
text: 6 very fresh free-range medium eggs, separated, plus 3 extra egg whites

label: OTHER
text: Chocolate curls (shaved from a block of chocolate with a peeler) to finish

label: OTHER
text: Melt the chocolate rn a heatproof bowl over a pan of simmering water, stirring every so often and making sure the bowl isn't touching the water. Remove from the heat and beat in the butter, using a whisk or spoon, until smooth beat the egg yolks in another bowl and set aside.

label: OTHER
text: In a clean, grease-free bowl , whrsk the egg whites until frothy but not stiff, using a mixer or an electric whisk on a medium-high speed. Add half the sugar

label: OTHER
text: And continue whisking on a low setting until stiff. Add the rest of the sugar and whisk until the egg whites stiffen up further. Now fold in the beaten egg yolks using a metal spoon

label: OTHER
text: Carefully stir half of this into the chocolate mixture, using a whisk, then fold in the rest with a large spoon until evenly combined. Pour into a large serving dish and leave to set for a couple of hours, or overnight.

label: OTHER
text: To serve, scoop a portion of mousse onto each serving plate and finish with chocolate curls

label: OTHER
text: A small piece of cinnamon stick

label: OTHER
text: A couple of handfuls of blackberries (optional)

label: OTHER
text: Clotted cream or ice cream to serve

label: OTHER
text: Peel the pears, leaving the stalks intact, and cut a thin sliver off the base of each one so they will stand upright.

label: OTHER
text: Put the perry, cloves, cinnamon, peppercorns and caster sugar into a heavy-based saucepan and add the pears. Lay a piece of greaseproof paper over the fruit. Bring to a simmer and poach gently for about 45 minutes until the pears are soft and tender but still holding their shape.

label: OTHER
text: Lift out the poached pears. Using a slotted spoon. And set aside on a plate.

label: OTHER
text: Continue to simmer the liquor in the pan until 1t has reduced by about two thirds and t111ckened return the pears to the liquor and leave to cool

label: OTHER
text: To serve, stand each pear 1n a deep scrvrng plate and spoon over some of the reduced liquor. Add a few blackberries, if using, and a scoop of clotted cream

label: OTHER
text: Rhubarb crumble and custard ice cream

label: OTHER
text: 400ml creamy milk, such as gold top, guernsey or jersey

label: OTHER
text: 1 1/2 vanilla pods or 1/2 tsp vanilla extract

label: OTHER
text: 400ml jersey or clotted cream, or a mixture

label: OTHER
text: 1 tsp cornflour, mixed with 2 tbsp cold water

label: OTHER
text: 80g cold butter, cut into small pieces

label: OTHER
text: 60-80g shelled nuts, such as walnuts, hazelnuts

label: OTHER
text: Pour the milk into a saucepan split the vanilla pods lengthways, scrape out the seeds with a knife and add them to the milk with the empty pods. (if using vanilla extract, don't add it yet.) Slowly bring the milk to the boil, then take the pan off the heat and remove the empty vanilla pods

label: OTHER
text: Whisk the egg yolks and sugar together in a bowl, then pour on the hot milk, whisking as you do so. Return to the pan and cook gently over a low heat, stirringconstantly with a wooden spoon, for about 5 minutes untilthe custard thickens; don't let it boil. Pour into a bowl and whisk in the cream (add the

label: OTHER
text: Vanilla extract now, if using.) Cover the surface with a sheet of greaseproof paper to prevent a skin forming and leave to cool

label: OTHER
text: Preheat the oven to 190"c/gas mark 5 . Cut the rhubarb roughly into 2cm pieces, place 1n an ovenproof dish and scatter with the sugar. Cover with foil and cook in the oven for about 30 minutes until tender. Pour the juice from the rhubarb into a saucepan and bring to a simmer stir the blended cornflour into the liquor and cook, stirring, for a minute or so to thicken, then stir back into the rhubarb.leave to cool.

label: OTHER
text: To make the crumble, put the flour into a bowl and rub in the butter until the mixture 1s the texture of breadcrumbs. Mix in the sugar,followed by the oats, nuts and pumpkin seeds. Scatter on a baking tray and bake for about 15 minutes until golden set aside to cool.

label: OTHER
text: Churn the custard in an ice-cream machine until thickened and almost frozen to the right consistency for serving, then fold in the rhubarb and half of the crumble mixture. Transfer to a suitable container and place in the freezer until needed. Keep the rest of the crumble in an airtight container

label: OTHER
text: To serve,scoop the ice cream into glass bowls and scatter the reserved crumble on top.

label: OTHER
text: 200g caster sugar, plus extra for dusting

label: OTHER
text: 500g brown or white bread, crusts removed

label: OTHER
text: 4 medium crisp dessert apples, such as cox's

label: OTHER
text: Thick jersey cream or ice cream to serve

label: OTHER
text: Put the cider, sugar, spices, sultanas, raisins and orange zest into a saucepan and bring to the boil. Meanwhile, break the bread into small pieces and place in a bowl tip the cider mixture over the bread and stir to mix. Cover the bowl with cling film and leave to stand overnight.

label: OTHER
text: The next day, peel, quarter, core and slice the apples.

label: OTHER
text: Melt the butter in a large frying pan and fry the apples over a medium heat, stirring occasionally, for 3-4 minutes until lightly coloured. Set aside to cool.

label: OTHER
text: Preheat the oven to 180°c. Line the base and sides or a 20cm square baking tin, about 6cm deep, with greaseproof paper.

label: OTHER
text: Fold the beaten eggs into the bread mixture, then fold in the cooled apples.spoon the mixture into the prepared tin and bake for about 30-40 minutes until firm dust the top with caster sugar while still hot. Serve with cream or ice cream

label: OTHER
text: Roast plums with cobnuts and clotted cream

label: OTHER
text: 12- 18 victoria or other plums, depending on size

label: OTHER
text: 40g hard butter, chopped into small pieces

label: OTHER
text: Clotted cream or thick jersey cream

label: OTHER
text: Preheat the oven to 200 c. First make the topping:put the flour and butter into a bowl and rub together with your fingers to a breadcrumb-like consistency. Stir in the brown sugar, then mix in the oats and cobnuts. Spread out on a baking tray.

label: OTHER
text: Halve the plums, place cut side up on another baking tray and sprinkle with the caster sugar

label: OTHER
text: Place both or the trays in the oven and bake for 15-20 minutes until the plums are lightly coloured and softened and the topping 1s golden; you may need to take the toppingout before the plums

label: OTHER
text: Leave the plums to cool slightly, then transfer them to individual serving bowls and spoon over the cooking juices.scatter over the cobnut topping and top with a generous dollop of cream

label: OTHER
text: 11og butter, softened , plus extra to grease

label: OTHER
text: 225g strong plain flour, plus extra for dusting

label: OTHER
text: 1 tsp arrowroot or cornflour, mixed with 1 tbsp

label: OTHER
text: 1 free-range medium egg white, mixed with 1 tbsp caster sugar

label: OTHER
text: First make the pastry. In a food processor or electric mixer, or by hand, cream the butter and sugar together untilsmooth. Sift the flour, salt and baking powder together and stir into the creamed mixture until evenly combined. Now slowly incorporate the cream until well mixed.

label: OTHER
text: Knead the dough lightly, then divide into 2 portions, one slightly bigger than the other. Shape each into a ball, wrap in clingfilm and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

label: OTHER
text: Lightly butter four locm individual tart tins, about 3cm deep. Roll out the slightly larger ball of pastry on a lightly floured surface to about a 3mm thickness. Using an l8cm plate as a guide,cut out 4 discs. Use to line the tart tins, leaving about 5mm pastry overhanging the edge.

label: OTHER
text: Roll out the other portion of pastry and cut 4 discs, a little more than locm in diameter, for the pie lids. Place on a tray. Put the lined tart tins and pastry discs in the fridge and leave to rest for 1hour.

label: OTHER
text: Meanwhile, to make the filling, put 200g of the blackberries into a saucepan with the sugar and 1 tbsp water. Bringto a simmer and cook gently for 3-4 minutes. Add the blended arrowroot and simmer for another 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally. Strain through a fine-meshed sieve into a bowl and leave to cool a little, then mix in the rest of the blackberries.

label: OTHER
text: Preheat the oven to 200°c. Remove the pastry from the fridge. Using a slotted spoon, fill the pastry cases with the blackberry filling. Moisten with the juice from the fruit , but don't add too much brush the pastry rim with water. Then lay the pastry lids over the pies, pressing the edges together to seal with your fingers.

label: OTHER
text: Brush the tops with the egg white and sugar mix. Then make a small slit in the centre with the point of a knife. Put the tart tins on a baking tray and bake in the oven for 20-25 minutes untilgolden,turning the oven down a little or covering loosely with foil if they appear to be colouring too quickly.

label: OTHER
text: Leave the pies to rest for about 15 minutes, then unmould onto plates. Dust with icing sugar and serve with a generous spoonful of thick cream or creme fralche.
